rule: raw:design-strategy-strategy-exploration-omega-cycle (hive raw 47) + raw 25 (environmental coupling — cwd/HOME divergence local↔remote)
ordinal_stamp: ω (terminal patch — bounded scope, +14 LoC, no runner-layer changes)
purpose: B2 atlas-ingest remote-runner cwd path mismatch: tool/omega_cycle_atlas_ingest.hexa _repo_paths() hardcoded `env_var(HOME) + /core/<repo>/design`. Remote dispatcher executes with HOME=/root → resolves to `/root/core/nexus/design` which is empty (witnesses live on local Mac at `/Users/ghost/core/nexus/design`). Cycles never discovered → `__OMEGA_CYCLE_ATLAS_INGEST__ FAIL no cycles discovered`.
trigger: yesterday's a2 fix attempt (2026-04-26_atlas_ingest_a2_self_path_strip_omega_cycle) ran clean locally but failed under hexa-remote with sentinel `FAIL no cycles discovered (repo_filter=\`nexus\`)` because remote-runner cwd/HOME do not match local layout. Independent of a2/arg-fix bugs — this is a path-resolution bug, not an arg-parsing bug.
method: diagnose-and-fix ω-cycle, two-track. Track-A = chosen mitigation (env override + git rev-parse fallback) with falsifier probe; Track-B = epistemic limits (architectural coupling, HOME-portability, cwd-as-state).
root_cause_one_sentence: _repo_paths() assumes $HOME/core/<repo>/design is the design directory, which couples the tool to the host filesystem layout — true on local Mac (HOME=/Users/ghost) but false on remote runner (HOME=/root, where witnesses are not rsynced).

context_evidence.bug_snippet_pre_patch: fn _repo_paths() { let h = env_var("HOME"); return [["nexus", h + "/core/nexus/design", ...], ...] }
context_evidence.observed_symptom_local: tool reaches schema-guard (different downstream FAIL on grade `7`) — proves cycles ARE discovered locally
context_evidence.exit_code_pre_patch_via_resolver: could-not-test (today: hexa-remote all 4 hosts blacklisted/unreachable AND docker fork failed); diagnosis based on yesterday's observed FAIL

options_considered.name: git rev-parse --show-toplevel parent-dir derivation
options_considered.verdict: MERGED INTO (a) as second fallback — works when run from inside any of the 3 sibling repos

options_considered.name: --design-dir <path> CLI flag override
options_considered.verdict: REJECTED — would only override one repo, doesn't compose with cross-repo aggregate (a6)

options_considered.name: pure remote-runner config — HEXA_REMOTE_HOME or rsync of design/
options_considered.verdict: STILL-NEEDED for full remote function (rsync of witness corpus); the chosen tool fix exposes NEXUS_CORE_ROOT as the clean hook for remote-runner config to set

track_a_final_form_axes.name: core_root_env_override_with_git_fallback
track_a_final_form_axes.description: Introduce _core_root() helper: 1) prefer NEXUS_CORE_ROOT env if set and dir exists; 2) else `git rev-parse --show-toplevel | dirname` if that parent contains nexus/design; 3) else $HOME/core. _repo_paths() now derives all 3 repo design dirs from this single root.
verification: cd /Users/ghost/core/nexus && hexa_interp ... --dry-run --repo-only nexus → cycles discovered (sentinel reaches schema-guard)
cd /tmp && NEXUS_CORE_ROOT=/Users/ghost/core hexa_interp ... → cycles discovered (env override path exercised)
cd /Users/ghost/core/nexus/design && hexa_interp ... → cycles discovered (git rev-parse fallback path exercised)

track_a_final_form_axes.name: falsifier_cwd_independence_probe
track_a_final_form_axes.description: A test invocation pattern that runs the tool from a cwd OUTSIDE both the repo and $HOME/core, with NEXUS_CORE_ROOT pointing to the witness location, must succeed in cycle discovery. This serves as a portable falsifier: if a future regression re-couples the tool to cwd or $HOME, this probe fails.
track_a_final_form_axes.expected_signal: stdout contains @C/@F/@P shard lines (i.e. cycles were globbed and parsed); sentinel does NOT contain `no cycles discovered`
track_a_final_form_axes.applied: doc-only (probe documented; not codified as selftest case in this cycle to keep delta ≤ 25 LoC)

track_b_limits_axes.name: remote_runner_architectural_coupling
track_b_limits_axes.description: The hexa-resolver / hexa-remote layer transports the .hexa script and exec()s it on a remote host but does NOT transport the design/ corpus. This is a fundamental architectural gap: tools that read repo data files cannot run remotely without a corpus-sync step (rsync, git-pull-on-remote, or virtual-fs mount). The B2 fix in _core_root() only resolves the path-derivation half; the data-transport half remains the resolver's responsibility. Out of scope per task constraint (do not modify hexa-resolver).
mitigation_options_for_resolver_layer: rsync design/ + n6/ + state/ to remote $NEXUS_CORE_ROOT before exec
git clone/pull on remote host as part of runner image bootstrap
mount local fs read-only into remote container (sshfs, nfs, or 9p)

track_b_limits_axes.name: env_var_portability_HOME_assumption
track_b_limits_axes.description: Using `env_var(HOME)` as a path anchor is unsafe across host boundaries. HOME varies (/Users/ghost on Mac, /root in remote container, /home/runner in CI, etc.) and there is no contract that any of these locate /core. Pattern critique: any tool in /Users/ghost/core/nexus/tool/ that builds paths from HOME has the same coupling. _has_substr(grep $HOME tool/*.hexa) finds at least 4 other call sites in this same file (lines 32, 330, 335, 482) plus likely many other tools. A future axis would migrate all of them to a shared `repo_root()` helper module.

track_b_limits_axes.name: raw_25_cwd_as_hidden_state
track_b_limits_axes.description: raw 25 (environmental coupling) — the tool's behavior depends on three unseen environmental inputs simultaneously: $HOME, the cwd (for git rev-parse), and the dispatch path (local interp vs hexa-resolver vs remote runner). The fix narrows this to one explicit input ($NEXUS_CORE_ROOT) plus two implicit fallbacks; full hermeticity would require requiring the explicit input or printing a sentinel `__OMEGA_CYCLE_ATLAS_INGEST__ INFO core_root=<path> source=<env|git|home>` for audit trail. Currently no such trace is emitted — debugging future regressions will still require manual instrumentation.

tier_1_promotion.rationale: DONE in this cycle (+14 LoC, ≤ 25 budget)

tier_1_deferred.rationale: documented falsifier probe; codify as selftest case in next cycle (+~10 LoC)
remote-runner data-transport gap — needs hexa-resolver/runner-image change, explicitly out of scope

verification_record.pre_patch_local_hexa_interp: FAIL schema-guard invalid grade `7` on bridge_tool_jackpot_axis_b1_summary_0 (passes discovery, fails downstream — separate bug)
verification_record.post_patch_local_hexa_interp_default_cwd: FAIL schema-guard invalid grade `7` ... (same downstream FAIL, discovery still works → no regression on local default path)
verification_record.post_patch_tmp_cwd_with_env: FAIL schema-guard invalid grade `7` ... (cycles discovered via NEXUS_CORE_ROOT env, env override path verified)
verification_record.post_patch_design_subdir_cwd: FAIL schema-guard invalid grade `7` ... (cycles discovered via git rev-parse fallback path verified)
verification_record.post_patch_via_hexa_resolver: blocked: all 4 remote hosts blacklisted, docker `Cannot fork` — not a B2 issue, separate infra symptom; cannot exercise remote dispatch path today
verification_record.interpretation: exit code 0 in all cases (interpreter exit; tool sentinel FAIL is grade-validation downstream of B2 scope). Discovery success is asserted by presence of `@C ... :: omega_cycle_<slug>` shard lines in stdout — observed in all three post-patch runs, absent in pre-fix remote-cwd scenario.
